title: lavandula pedunculata subsp. pedunculata
intro: Lavandula pedunculata subsp. pedunculata, also known as the tree lavender or Provence lavender, is a species of lavender native to the Mediterranean region. It is a shrub-like plant known for its grayish-green leaves and purple flowers, which possess a range of medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Lavandula pedunculata subsp. pedunculata include its ability to aid in relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, and improve sleep quality, as well as its potential to alleviate digestive issues and soothe skin irritations. Lavandula pedunculata subsp. pedunculata can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, essential oils, and topical creams, making it a versatile and valuable addition to traditional medicine.
